settings.title: Price Watch — first-time setup
settings.description:
Price Watch can extract product info two ways:

1. **Free, no API needed** — works on any site with Schema.org Product markup (most major retailers), or via a custom parser you define per-site.

2. **AI extraction** — works on any site. Pick a provider below:

• **Anthropic (Claude)** — paste an `sk-ant-…` key. Typical cost: $0.50–$2/month for ~10 products. See {docs_url}.
• **OpenAI-compatible** — point at any service speaking OpenAI's chat-completions API: OpenAI itself, Ollama (free, local), Groq, OpenRouter, LM Studio, Together, Fireworks, etc. Fill in `base_url` (e.g. `https://api.openai.com/v1` or `http://localhost:11434/v1`) and `model`. Leave the API key blank for local servers.

Leave both API keys blank to start in free mode. You can change provider/keys later in the integration's options.

Set a home currency (3-letter code like ISK, EUR, USD) to enable the price-in-local-currency sensor. Supported: AUD BGN BRL CAD CHF CNY CZK DKK EUR GBP HKD HUF IDR ILS INR ISK JPY KRW MXN MYR NOK NZD PHP PLN RON SEK SGD THB TRY USD ZAR.

data.api_key: API key (Anthropic: sk-ant-… / OpenAI: sk-… / local: leave blank)
data.model_anthropic: Anthropic model (only matters when provider = Anthropic)
data.base_url: OpenAI-compat base URL (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1 or http://localhost:11434/v1)
data.model_openai: OpenAI-compat model name (e.g. gpt-4o-mini, llama3.3, qwen-coder)
data.input_cost_per_mtok: Input cost per million tokens (USD, 0 = free/unknown)
data.output_cost_per_mtok: Output cost per million tokens (USD, 0 = free/unknown)
data.max_html_chars: Max HTML chars sent to model (smaller for tiny local models)
data.force_json_mode: Force JSON mode instead of tool calls (older Ollama, some local models)
data.extra_headers: Extra HTTP headers (optional JSON object, e.g. for OpenRouter analytics)

product.title: Add a product to track
product.description:
Paste the product URL. Price Watch will fetch the page and extract the product details.

**Cookies (optional)** — only needed for sites with hostile bot detection (Amazon, etc.). Open the product page in your browser, then DevTools (F12) → Network tab → reload → click the document request → Headers → copy the `Cookie:` value and paste it below.

data.cookies: Cookies (optional — for sites that need them)

confirm.title: Confirm tracked product

user.title: Add a tracked product
user.description: How do you want to add this product?

menu_options.product: Add by URL (paste a product link, integration fetches details)
menu_options.shell: Add by name (create empty entry, add retailer URLs later)

shell.title: Add product (shell mode)
shell.description:
Create an empty tracked product — just the name, no URL yet.

After creation, add retailer URLs using the **price_watch.add_listing** service. Each listing gets its own price/lowest/highest sensors and polls independently. This is useful when you want to track the same product across multiple retailers.

error.extraction_failed: Could not extract product info from this URL. The page may be JS-rendered or protected. Try defining a custom parser in advanced options.
error.needs_api_or_parser: This page has no Schema.org Product data, so it can't be tracked in free mode. Either add an Anthropic API key in the integration options, or define a custom parser for this site (see docs).
error.validation_error: Could not validate the API key. Check your network connection.
error.base_url_required: Base URL is required for OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1).
error.model_required: Model name is required for OpenAI-compatible providers.
error.openai_compat_unreachable: Could not reach the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Check the URL, network, and that the server is running. If the server doesn't support tool calls, try the 'Force JSON mode' option.
error.extra_headers_invalid_json: Extra headers must be valid JSON (e.g. {"HTTP-Referer": "https://myapp.com"}).
error.extra_headers_not_object: Extra headers must be a JSON object (key/value pairs), not an array or string.
error.custom_parser_invalid_json: Custom parser must be valid JSON (e.g. {"type": "regex", "price": "\\$([\\d.,]+)"}).
error.custom_parser_not_object: Custom parser must be a JSON object (key/value pairs), not an array or string.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. See HA logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This URL is already being tracked.

init.title: Price Watch options
init.description: Choose what to configure. Each section has its own page so you only see what's relevant. Click **Save and exit** when you're done.

menu_options.basic: Basic — target price, check interval, pause
menu_options.parser: Parser — cookies and custom parser config

data.api_key: API key (Anthropic: sk-ant-… / OpenAI: sk-… / local: leave blank)
data.max_html_chars: Max HTML chars sent to model
data.force_json_mode: Force JSON mode (older Ollama / some local models)

basic.title: Basic options
basic.description: Target price, how often to check, and whether to pause polling.

data.back_to_menu: ← Back to menu (discard changes on this page)

parser.title: Parser options
parser.description:
Cookies and custom parser config. Cookies are stored inside the parser config; leave the parser field alone unless you know what you're doing.

**Cookies** — paste a Cookie header value to send with every request (DevTools → Network tab → reload → click document → Headers → copy `Cookie:` value).

**Custom parser** — raw JSON, e.g. `{"type": "regex", "price": "\\$([\\d.,]+)", "request_cookies": "..."}`. Leave blank to use the auto-detected preset.

data.back_to_menu: ← Back to menu (discard changes on this page)

provider.title: AI provider override
provider.description:
Override the shared AI provider for this product only. Default is **Inherit from settings** — leave it there unless you have a specific reason to use a different provider/model for this URL.

Fields not relevant to the chosen provider are ignored.

data.max_html_chars: Max HTML chars sent to model
data.back_to_menu: ← Back to menu (discard changes on this page)

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.description:
Manual overrides. Use sparingly.

**Force discontinued** — manually mark this product as discontinued. Polling stops and the sensor shows the last known price. Untick to resume polling.

**Reset history** — wipe the price history (lowest/highest/sparkline). Useful after fixing a parser that was producing bad prices. The current price is kept. This is a one-shot action; the checkbox doesn't persist.

data.back_to_menu: ← Back to menu (discard changes on this page)
